flags.concern: Agent task lineage: the plan only wires URL-based params into `create-task.ts`, but the in-app create-task callers for img2img, magic edit, image upscale, and video enhance also pass source generation lineage (`based_on` or `generation_id`). Without carrying `selectedClips[*].generation_id` through the agent path, these new tasks will queue but lose the parent-generation linkage and shot-context behavior the app already relies on.
flags.evidence: Revised to address FLAG-001 (generation lineage). Key changes: (1) Added `based_on` field to `CreateGenerationTaskArgs` interface in Step 2. (2) Each new builder function now includes lineage fields matching what the in-app hooks pass — `based_on` for img2img/magic-edit/video-enhance, `generation_id` for upscale. (3) Step 3 now extracts `sourceGenerationId` from `selectedReferenceClips[0]?.generation_id` and passes it through to `createGenerationTask`. (4) Added lineage verification to test criteria in Step 5. (5) Updated Validation Order to include lineage field check. `source_variant_id` is documented as an acceptable omission since `SelectedClipPayload` doesn't carry variant info.

flags.concern: Travel model surface: the plan does not actually mirror the app’s image-to-video model contract. The repository exposes `wan-2.2`, `ltx-2.3`, and `ltx-2.3-fast`, with model-specific defaults in `modelCapabilities.ts`, but the plan introduces a generic `wan` enum value and limits the `modelCapabilities.ts` check to name mapping. That will leave the agent on a different public model ID surface than the app and still won’t apply the model-specific defaults the file exists to define.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/travel-between-images/modelCapabilities.ts:4-5` defines the actual selectable IDs as `['wan-2.2', 'ltx-2.3', 'ltx-2.3-fast']`, and `src/tools/travel-between-images/modelCapabilities.ts:80-118` attaches per-model defaults such as `defaultWorkerModelName`, `defaultFrames`, and `defaultSteps`. The plan’s Step 1 enum uses `wan` instead of `wan-2.2`, and Step 2 says to use `modelCapabilities.ts` only for mapping `ltx-2.3` / `ltx-2.3-fast` to worker names.

flags.concern: Character animate defaults: the plan invents a `720p` default for the agent builder, but the existing app flow and helper both default this task to `480p`. That changes the behavior and cost of the path the plan is supposed to mirror.
flags.evidence: The proposed Step 2 builder sets `resolution: "720p"`. In the repository, `src/tools/character-animate/lib/characterAnimate.ts:26-32` defines the default resolution as `480p`, and `src/tools/character-animate/hooks/useCharacterAnimateGenerate.ts:48-56` explicitly submits `resolution: '480p'`.

flags.concern: Agent generation verification: the plan’s test step does not use the existing harness that already targets timeline-agent generation flows, even though the user explicitly asked for harness testing. That leaves the most relevant regression surface out of the planned verification.
flags.evidence: `config/testing/vitest.edge.smoke.config.ts:4-7` includes `supabase/functions/_tests/harness/**/*.test.ts`, `supabase/functions/_tests/harness/runner.ts:144-183` provides a reusable harness runner for agent messages plus selected clips, and `supabase/functions/_tests/harness/diverse-gen-test.ts:1-5` is specifically described as exercising every generation type the timeline agent supports. The plan’s Step 5 only says to search for tests and then do manual verification via deployed chat.
